author: Jamie Silva
description: These chewy chocolate chip cookies have ooey-gooey centers, perfectly crisp edges, and melty chunks of dark chocolate. Made easily in one bowl, they can be baked right away or frozen for cookies on demand.
ingredients: 1 cup chopped dark chocolate (or chocolate of choice)
instructions: In a large bowl or mixer, combine melted butter and both sugars. Mix until smooth.
Add the egg and vanilla extract and mix until fully incorporated.
Add flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix just until combined; do not overmix.
Using a cookie scoop, form dough into balls (about 1 1/2 tablespoons each).
Refrigerate dough balls for at least 20 minutes, or freeze for later use.
When ready to bake, preheat oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place dough balls on the baking sheet and bake for 10-11 minutes, until edges are lightly golden.
Remove from the oven, sprinkle with flaky sea salt if desired, and let cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Cookies may appear slightly underbaked when removed; they will continue to set as they cool.
Dough can be frozen for up to 3 months and baked directly from frozen with an extra minute or two added to bake time.
Do not overmix the dough to keep cookies soft and chewy.
title: The Best Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies
